__inputs.name: VAR_BLOCKY_URL
__inputs.label: blocky API URL
__inputs.description: Base URL of the blocky HTTP API, used by the blocking control links

description: Operational dashboard for the blocky DNS proxy and ad blocker: traffic, latency, blocking effectiveness, caching, DNSSEC, rate limiting and Go runtime health.

panels.title: Blocking
panels.description: Ad/tracker blocking status. Use the 'Blocking control' buttons below to enable or temporarily disable blocking via the blocky API.

panels.title: Query rate
panels.description: Current DNS queries per second across all selected instances.

panels.title: Blocked
panels.description: Share of queries answered as BLOCKED over the dashboard time range.

panels.title: p95 latency
panels.description: 95th percentile of request duration (all response types).

panels.title: Cache hit ratio
panels.description: Share of cacheable queries answered from the result cache.

panels.title: Errors
panels.description: Queries that ended in an error over the dashboard time range.

panels.title: Uptime
panels.description: Time since the (longest running) blocky process started.

panels.title: Denylist entries
panels.description: Total domains in all denylist groups (summed over instances).

panels.title: Last list refresh
panels.description: Age of the newest deny/allowlist refresh. Turns yellow after one day and red after three days without a refresh.

panels.title: Cache entries
panels.description: Entries currently in the DNS result cache (summed over instances).

panels.title: Failed list downloads
panels.description: Deny/allowlist download failures over the dashboard time range.

panels.title: Blocking control
panels.description: Enable or temporarily disable blocking via the blocky API ($blocky_url). Grafana shows a notification with the result; the 'Blocking' stat and the 'Blocking active' timeline reflect the change on the next refresh.

panels.title: Queries by outcome
panels.description: Stacked query rate split by how blocky answered: from cache, blocked, resolved upstream, custom DNS, etc.

panels.title: Query rate by client (top 10)
panels.description: The ten busiest clients over time.

panels.title: Top clients
panels.description: Clients with the most queries over the dashboard time range.

panels.title: Query types
panels.description: DNS record types requested (A, AAAA, PTR, …).

panels.title: Response codes
panels.description: DNS response codes returned to clients (NOERROR, NXDOMAIN, SERVFAIL, …).

panels.title: Response reasons
panels.description: Detailed answer reason, including the upstream resolver or the denylist group that matched.

panels.title: Request duration percentiles
panels.description: Median, 90th and 99th percentile of end-to-end request duration.

panels.title: Request duration heatmap
panels.description: Distribution of request durations over time.

panels.title: p95 duration by outcome
panels.description: p95 duration per response type — cached answers should be microseconds, RESOLVED reflects upstream latency.

panels.title: Blocking active
panels.description: History of the blocking switch — shows when blocking was temporarily disabled.

panels.title: Blocked queries
panels.description: Rate of blocked queries (left axis) and blocked share of all queries (right axis).

panels.title: Top block reasons
panels.description: Which denylist groups blocked the most queries in the dashboard time range.

panels.title: Failed list downloads
panels.description: List download failures over time — investigate sustained failures (lists go stale).

panels.title: Cache size
panels.description: Entries in the result cache and number of domains tracked as prefetch candidates.

panels.title: Prefetch activity
panels.description: Domains refreshed ahead of expiry and answers served from prefetched entries.

panels.title: Redis buffer drops
panels.description: Cache writes dropped because the Redis write-through buffer was full. Only relevant with Redis enabled; non-zero values mean Redis cannot keep up.

panels.title: Validations by result
panels.description: DNSSEC validation outcomes. A rise in 'bogus' results means responses failed signature validation.

panels.title: Dropped queries by protocol
panels.description: Queries rejected by the per-client rate limiter.

panels.title: Active buckets
panels.description: Token buckets (≈ distinct clients) currently tracked in memory.

panels.title: Bucket store cap exhausted
panels.description: Queries dropped because the bucket store hit its size cap — consider raising the cap if this fires under normal load.

title: Blocky
